compiled_state.title: LLM agent-operations framework (rebar.llm) + reference review op
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context
rebar needs first-class, reliable LLM "agent operations": tool-using agents that
review tickets/code and emit structured, schema-validated findings, exposed over
the same three interfaces as everything else (library, CLI, MCP). The agent
loop is a solved problem, so we build on an existing runtime rather than
reinventing one, and we must support filesystem + MCP tools and configurable
Langflow/Langfuse across environments (some of which cannot run Langflow).

## What / Scope
- An optional `rebar.llm` framework: pluggable Runner protocol (default in-process
  LangChain/LangGraph; Langflow REST; Fake for tests), reviewer registry (Langfuse
  prompt management + packaged fallbacks), and a findings contract pinned to the
  `review_result` JSON Schema.
- A reference operation `review_ticket` wired across library / CLI (`rebar review`)
  / MCP (`review_ticket`, gated).
- Optionality is a hard rule: core rebar stays stdlib-only; the stack is behind
  `nava-rebar[agents]` and lazy-imported.

## Success Criteria
- The framework ships and dogfoods: agents can review rebar tickets via rebar.
- Adding a new operation or reviewer is a small, documented change.
- Works across providers (Anthropic default; OpenAI/Gemini/local) and degrades
  cleanly when extras/credentials are absent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] `rebar.llm` framework + `review_ticket` shipped over library/CLI/MCP
- [x] `review_result` schema + finding/citation/severity shared $defs, wired into OUTPUT_SCHEMAS
- [x] Offline test coverage (FakeRunner) + import-cleanliness guard; no interfaces-tier regressions
- [x] Docs: docs/llm-framework.md + README/CLAUDE.md/architecture.md
- [ ] Follow-up child tickets (Langflow client, code-review/aggregation, batch scan, live validation) tracked

comments.body: Review findings (2 Opus agents + OSS-gotchas research) resolved, and multi-provider config landed: the [agents] extra now ships Claude + ChatGPT, provider chosen per run via REBAR_LLM_MODEL/_MODEL_PROVIDER/_BASE_URL. Validated in-session that the full agent assembles for both ChatAnthropic and ChatOpenAI (commits 7db1008, prior (llm) commits).
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: DeepAgentsRunner landed as an OPT-IN runner (REBAR_LLM_RUNNER=deepagents): runs the operation on LangChain deepagents (planning/subagents/eviction) with a read-only repo-rooted FilesystemBackend + our findings schema. The review DEFAULT stays the langgraph runner with our citation-disciplined tools; deepagents is the seam for future task types. (commit pending)
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Follow-ups implemented: LangflowRunner REST client (0224 closed), code-review op + multi-reviewer aggregation + code-quality reviewer (47fb closed), batch epic-vs-spec scan + spec-alignment reviewer (453c closed). Live-runtime validation harness landed (b2e5, left in_progress — needs an actual run with API keys). New surface: rebar.llm.review_code/scan_epics_for_spec + CLI rebar review-code/scan-spec + gated MCP review_code/scan_spec. All offline-tested via FakeRunner; review default stays the langgraph runner.
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Epic complete (2026-06-15). All five children closed (0224 langflow client, 47fb code-review+aggregation, 453c batch spec-scan, e300 framework+review_ticket, b2e5 live-validation). The hard optionality rule is now VALIDATED, not just asserted: tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_optionality.py exhaustively proves the rebar.llm stack is optional across all three interfaces (library/CLI/MCP) x all three operations (review_ticket/review_code/scan_epics_for_spec) — import-cleanliness + clean degradation (typed LLMError / CLI Error:+exit1 / gated MCP tool error) when the agents extra is absent. A real silent-success bug (scan over zero epics never invoked the runner) was found by subagent review and fixed via an offline Runner.preflight() readiness check (now in scan_epics_for_spec + review_code). Core rebar stays stdlib-only and lazy-imports the stack. Live-model runtime checks (b2e5) remain a key-gated CI dispatch (external-integration.yml). Full non-integration suite green: 1829 passed, 7 skipped. Commit 045b2d77.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
